# Transcrição: 2. CAPI- Aggregated Events Manager (AEM) Set Up

- **Fonte:** 2. CAPI- Aggregated Events Manager (AEM) Set Up
- **Duração:** 04:01
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

alongside having the conversion API set up is making sure aggregated event measurement AEM is set up as well. So in the same way you go into your tools menu, events manager, go into your pixel into overview and rather than sitting on pixel conversions API, click into aggregated event measurement and manage events. So what you want to do here is find your domain, click it and then click on manage events. If you cannot see, if the link is grayed out for manage events or you cannot see the domain here, for example, you work with clients, that means you haven't been given permission in the business manager for that ad account to be able to view the domain and you need to get access to that. So whether it's your own domain or a client's make sure it is set up correctly. So I'm going to click edit just to show you a typical setup for Econ. So what we have here are five events. One of the events has been set up for value optimization. So therefore it takes up four slots of your allocated A. So what this says is if someone's on iOS 14, or for whatever reason they've blocked access to view the data and tracking, we will instruct Facebook to pull out the highest value pixel because that's all we're allowed to see. So based on iOS 14 changes, what Facebook set up was, okay, for someone that's opted out, we can't see their full journey, but we can track what they eventually did. So our highest priority is purchase for Ecommerce funnel here. Next one down is add payment info, initiate checkout to cart and view content. Now, because on this account with selected value optimization, it takes up four of those eight slots. So I've only got four events left. If I chose not to do that, then I can add more events. And you can see here it says five, eight, then I can click add event, choose my pixel from here, click on the event, and I can add more here. But for this purpose, for this website, we're using value optimization. And what that means is it will look at value of the purchases and you can do a Rora space bid in and things like that, beyond just tracking the pixel event so we can now optimize for value. So higher average order values, for example, this is more applicable when your ecommerce store has multiple products and multiple prices. If your e-commerce store only has very few products, and your average order value is pretty much, you know, the same maybe only people buy one of a product, maybe your product, you know, selling a $200 product, people only really ever buy one product, then you don't really need value optimization. But in most cases, it's ideal to have that switched on.

## [03:00 - 04:01]

So that's really what you want to make sure we set. If you do make a change and apply it, it can take two to three days for it to kick in. So be aware of that. And then once you've got data coming through, it will show in terms of your highest priorities, how much data has actually come through. So in this view as an example, so this is another store that also has the value optimization set up for purchase. You can see that although we've set up purchase to be the highest value or highest priority event in AEM, the number of hits received for view content and add to cart is a lot higher. And then we can expand down to see what that data collection looks like over a period of time as well. And what they what you can see is when those events are coming through and how many of those events are being fired as well. So it's just to make sure that everything's working as far as the pixel is concerned.
